transliteration: duḥkheṣv anudvigna-manāḥ sukheṣu vigata-spṛhaḥ | vīta-rāga-bhaya-krodhaḥ sthita-dhīr munir ucyate ||
literal_meaning: In sorrows—undisturbed-in-mind; in pleasures—without-craving; freed-from attachment-fear-anger; steady-in-wisdom—such a sage is called.
contextual_meaning: Krishna sketches the emotional profile of a person whose mind is no longer jerked around by the push-pull of pleasure and pain.  Equanimity is not numbness; it is the capacity to feel fully without being colonized by the feeling.
psychological_state: Optimal arousal zone—neither hyper- nor hypo-aroused; pre-frontal cortex on-line while limbic spikes are witnessed, not enacted.
trigger_scenario: Stock-market crash wipes out half your retirement fund; you notice the surge of panic, breathe through it, and make decisions from values rather than visceral fear.
